title: Ixeridium laevigatum
intro: Ixeridium laevigatum, also known as Chilean yerba, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. Traditionally, Ixeridium laevigatum has been used in folk medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its reported health benefits of reducing oxidative stress and modulating the immune system. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Ixeridium laevigatum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties.
